subtitle: Slate-and-schist sea arches you can only walk among at low tide, on a Galician coast that rations summer visitors to 4,812 a day.

levers.photo_tone: wet dark slate, low-tide reflection, overcast Cantabrian light

spike_statement: The sea cut 30-metre arches into slate and schist here; you can only walk among them at low tide, and since 2015 summer entry is capped and permitted.
